name: flow-canvas-01
title: Flow Canvas 01
author: ilinxa
description: Node-and-edge canvas with typed ports, three keystone registries (renderer / port-type / edge-type), recursive sub-object rendering, and JSON-based save/load — built on @xyflow/react. Every node carries a __type field that keys into a renderer registry; unknown shapes fall back to a built-in custom-JSON renderer. Ports live inside the node data (recursively) so sub-objects can be extracted as standalone nodes through the same drop pipeline. Pan / zoom / fit-to-view, gradient background, controlled + uncontrolled state, imperative export ref, typed connection validation, multi-edge per port, sub-object drag-extract, read-only mode preserving pan / zoom / select.

files.content:
/* flow-canvas-01 selection ring — driven by xyflow's wrapper class. * * Decouples the selection VISUAL from any React-side render of NodeShell / * NodeAdapter / the consumer renderer. React-side re-renders on selection * still happen (xyflow flips the `selected` prop on NodeAdapter, which * recreates children) — this rule's win is that the visual ring applies * as a pure browser-side CSS effect the instant xyflow toggles the * `.selected` class, decoupled from React reconciliation timing. v0.2.0 * Tier 2 plan §5.3 + F-V1. * * DOM hierarchy when a node is selected: *
← xyflow's wrapper *
← NodeShell * ← ring target * ? ← also rings (matches v0.1.x) *
*
* * The `> * > *` selector targets NodeShell's direct children — the same * elements v0.1.x ringed via NodeShell's `data-[selected=true]:[&>*]:ring-2`. * * NO `border-radius: inherit` (plan F-V6): the renderer outputs already * carry their own border-radius (e.g. `rounded-md` on CustomJsonNode); * `box-shadow` automatically conforms to the element's own border-radius. * Setting `border-radius: inherit` here would pull NodeShell's (none) and * OVERRIDE the renderer's rounded corners. Don't. */ .react-flow__node.selected > * > * { box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px var(--ring); }
